author: @tannerbrettellmc-diy-er8840
text: Do you have to have the ferromagnetic core or is that just for the convenience mounting the inductor?

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: +Tanner Brettell Mc-DIY-er you can use steel - i used that because it was readily available

author: @tannerbrettellmc-diy-er8840
text: Interesting. I wonder if the bass is made more audible by the size of the membrane or by the power sent through the coil. I'm curious to try this with a hand wound coil. What gauge wire do you recommend?

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: +Tanner Brettell Mc-DIY-er try a thin one - to run this straight from a standard amplifier you will need to match the impedance

author: @mwm2929
text: Reminded me of when I was 12,I built speakers out of yogurt cups with some handmade coils of telephone wire. I think they were about 20 wraps.

author: @stefdijksman
text: Hey! i will make a video of it when i get the chance of purchasing the stuff i need! Thank you for the info. I will certainly look up the uk website (I live in Holland but online shops probably ship it to here)

author: @ThinkingandTinkering
text: I got mine from an old tv circuit board but you can buy them from radio shack if you are in the USA of RSonline if you are in the UK. You could try Maplins too. You can make your own from a short piece of ferrite rod and some magnet wire - if i was to rebuild this i would probably go that way - I would also try a toroid. Never done it myself but the results could be interesting - good luck and post a few vids i'd like to see how you get on

author: @stefdijksman
text: thats awesome! I really would love to make that! where can i get that kind of a induction coil? (i'm kind of new in coils and magnets and stuff but i'm interested in free energy and just starting to teach myself the basics atm)

author: @ThinkingandTinkering
text: Thanks for your comment - no not yet - i have been working on quite a few things recently - it all takes so much time lol - to be honest i doubt i will do anymore with this if you want to give a go and give me some data feedback i'd be really interested.

author: @ThinkingandTinkering
text: lol - you are a star lol - cheers!

author: @iluzyon1
text: Lolll that cool...thanks for showing it! Now, your next video ;)
